사무엘상 18:1.content: When he had finished speaking to Saul, the soul of Jonathan was knit with the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as his own soul.

사무엘상 18:2.content: Saul took him that day, and wouldn’t let him go home to his father’s house any more.

사무엘상 18:3.content: Then Jonathan and David made a covenant, because he loved him as his own soul.

사무엘상 18:4.content: Jonathan stripped himself of the robe that was on him, and gave it to David, and his clothing, even including his sword, his bow, and his sash.

사무엘상 18:5.content: David went out wherever Saul sent him, and behaved himself wisely; and Saul set him over the men of war. It was good in the sight of all the people, and also in the sight of Saul’s servants.

사무엘상 18:6.content: As they came, when David returned from the slaughter of the Philistine, the women came out of all the cities of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet king Saul, with tambourines, with joy, and with instruments of music.

사무엘상 18:7.content: The women sang to one another as they played, and said, “Saul has slain his thousands, and David his ten thousands.”

사무엘상 18:8.content: Saul was very angry, and this saying displeased him. He said, “They have creditd David with ten thousands, and they have only credited me with thousands. What can he have more but the kingdom?”

사무엘상 18:9.content: Saul watched David from that day and forward.

사무엘상 18:10.content: On the next day, an evil spirit from God came mightily on Saul, and he prophesied in the middle of the house. David played with his hand, as he did day by day. Saul had his spear in his hand;

사무엘상 18:11.content: and Saul threw the spear, for he said, “I will pin David to the wall!” David escaped from his presence twice.

사무엘상 18:12.content: Saul was afraid of David, because Yahweh was with him, and had departed from Saul.

사무엘상 18:13.content: Therefore Saul removed him from his presence, and made him his captain over a thousand; and he went out and came in before the people.

사무엘상 18:14.content: David behaved himself wisely in all his ways; and Yahweh was with him.

사무엘상 18:15.content: When Saul saw that he behaved himself very wisely, he stood in awe of him.

사무엘상 18:16.content: But all Israel and Judah loved David; for he went out and came in before them.

사무엘상 18:17.content: Saul said to David, “Behold, my elder daughter Merab, I will give her to you as wife. Only be valiant for me, and fight Yahweh’s battles.” For Saul said, “Don’t let my hand be on him, but let the hand of the Philistines be on him.”

사무엘상 18:18.content: David said to Saul, “Who am I, and what is my life, or my father’s family in Israel, that I should be son-in-law to the king?”

사무엘상 18:19.content: But at the time when Merab, Saul’s daughter, should have been given to David, she was given to Adriel the Meholathite as wife.

사무엘상 18:20.content: Michal, Saul’s daughter, loved David; and they told Saul, and the thing pleased him.

사무엘상 18:21.content: Saul said, I will give her to him, that she may be a snare to him, and that the hand of the Philistines may be against him. Therefore Saul said to David, “You shall today be my son-in-law a second time.”

사무엘상 18:22.content: Saul commanded his servants, “Talk with David secretly, and say, ‘Behold, the king has delight in you, and all his servants love you. Now therefore be the king’s son-in-law.’ ”

사무엘상 18:23.content: Saul’s servants spoke those words in the ears of David. David said, “Does it seem to you a light thing to be the king’s son-in-law, since I am a poor man, and little known?”

사무엘상 18:24.content: The servants of Saul told him, saying, “David spoke like this.”

사무엘상 18:25.content: Saul said, “Tell David, ‘The king desires no dowry except one hundred foreskins of the Philistines, to be avenged of the king’s enemies.’ ” Now Saul thought he would make David fall by the hand of the Philistines.

사무엘상 18:26.content: When his servants told David these words, it pleased David well to be the king’s son-in-law. Before the deadline,

사무엘상 18:27.content: David arose and went, he and his men, and killed two hundred men of the Philistines. Then David brought their foreskins, and they gave them in full number to the king, that he might be the king’s son-in-law. Then Saul gave him Michal his daughter as wife.

사무엘상 18:28.content: Saul saw and knew that Yahweh was with David; and Michal, Saul’s daughter, loved him.

사무엘상 18:29.content: Saul was even more afraid of David; and Saul was David’s enemy continually.

사무엘상 18:30.content: Then the princes of the Philistines went out; and as often as they went out, David behaved himself more wisely than all the servants of Saul, so that his name was highly esteemed.
